title: Animal Adoption - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Animal Adoption 37 Matching Service Providers ANIMAL AID INC DOMESTIC ANIMAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a free-roam shelter for cats and a foster program for cats and dogs. Also has cat adoptions and foster-to-adopt dogs. Public surrender intakes are considered on a case by case basis. 5335 SW 42nd Avenue Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Visiting hours for adoptions: Monday-Friday 11am-4pm, Saturday noon-4pm Phone: Monday-Friday 11am-4pm. Voicemails and callbacks are not returned on evenings and weekends. Email (503) 292-6628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. 7 E George Street Walla Walla, WA 99362 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Friday noon-6pm, Saturday noon-4pm Email (509) 525-2452 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter and adoption services for displaced animals, microchipping services, and a pet food bank that people can access every 3 months. Also sells Deschutes County dog licenses at cost. This is a no-kill shelter. All animals have been spayed and neutered. 1355 NE Hemlock Avenue Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday 11am-5:30pm, Thursday 11am-5pm Email (541) 923-0882 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAT ADOPTION TEAM PET ADOPTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides adoption services for cats and kittens. 14175 SW Galbreath Drive Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: -Age 18 and older -Ability to have an indoor-only pet -Agree to adoption policy which can be viewed online and at the shelter -An adult whom the cat will be living with must meet with an adoption counselor prior to adoption Hours: Shelter: Tuesday-Saturday noon-6pm (adoptions end 30 minutes before closing) Closed: New Year’s Day, Easter Sunday, Memorial Day, Fourth of July, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day Email (503) 925-8903 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY DOG SERVICES DOG SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides adoption of dogs, picks up strays, handles nuisance complaints, and provides licensing services. Also offers microchipping and rabies vaccinations for licensed dogs. 13141 SE Highway 212 Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Administration: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Adoption Viewing: Tuesday-Friday 1pm-4pm Email (503) 655-8628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ANIMAL CONTROL AND SHELTER ANIMAL CONTROL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides care and protection for homeless, unwanted, abandoned, abused, or neglected dogs and cats. Responds to complaints of stray and nuisance dogs, dog bites, and reports of animal abuse. Also offers cat and dog adoptions. 1315 SE 19th Street Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 9:30am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-4pm (503) 861-0737 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA GORGE HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. The society also has a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 200 River Road The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Age 21 or older to adopt Hours: Tuesday-Friday 11am-5pm, Saturday 10am-4pm, by appointment only Email (541) 296-5189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, animal shelter services, and animal surrender.This is a no-kill shelter. 2084 Oregon Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Saturday/Sunday noon-5pm Email (503) 397-4353 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cat and dog services including adoption, foster and volunteer opportunities, an events calendar, and pet resources. Accepts donations from their wish list page as well as those of a monetary nature. 1875 Highway 99 N Suite B-10 Ashland, OR 975359752 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Ashland office phone: Tuesday-Friday noon-4pm Donation drop off: Monday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 708-0090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoptions; reunification services; emergency boarding in crisis situations such as domestic violence, medical emergencies, disaster response, and other situations determined to be such; spay/neuter services for feral cats; a pet food pantry; foster care; humane education; dog licenses; and volunteer opportunities. 88530 Green Hill Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 11am-6pm Email (541) 689-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-door admission, low-cost adoptions, Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) for feral cats, and working cat adoptions for shelter animals. Offers lost and found pet assistance, a pet food pantry, low-cost vaccine clinics, a safe housing program for pets of people in crisis, and low-cost end-of-life services for the community. 398 SW Twin Oaks Circle Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday noon-6pm, Saturday-Sunday noon-5pm Email (541) 757-9000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HIGH PLATEAU HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed or neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. The society also has a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 114 E First Street Alturas, CA 96101 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday-Friday 10am-2pm, Saturday 10am-1pm Email (530) 233-9277 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEWARD BOUND PETS HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A no-kill shelter where community members can adopt animals. All dogs and cats have been spayed and neutered, are up-to-date on vaccines and have been treated for fleas and dewormed. All cats and kittens have been treated for ear mites and tested for Feline Leukemia and Feline AIDS. 10601 SE Loop Road Dayton, OR 97114 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Summer Hours (March-October): Thursday-Saturday 10am-5pm Winter Hours (October-March): Thursday-Saturday 10am-4pm Email (503) 472-0341 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE SHERIFF'S OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides law enforcement services that include search and rescue operations, criminal investigations, public records requests, and firearm licensing. Also provides animal-based services including animal control, cat and dog licensing, and shelter information. 309 State Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 386-2711 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet adoptions (all of which have been spayed and neutered), shelter, lost and found reporting program, HOPE Pet Food Bank, community cat program, and cremation services. Also offers Deschutes County dog licensing and renewal. Spay Neuter Assistance Program (SNAP) vouchers available that can be used at several different veterinary offices. 61170 SE 27th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 10am-5:30pm, additional times may be available by appointment Email (541) 382-3537 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HUMANE SOCIETY OF EASTERN OREGON PET RESCUE ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers animal adoption, shelter services, and animal surrender. 1844 NW Geer Road Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 11am-3pm (closed major holidays) Email (541) 564-6222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a shelter for lost, stray, abandoned, or unwanted animals. Offers pet licensing, regulation compliance, and pet adoption. Accepts reports for animal bites. 5595 S Pacific Highway Phoenix, OR 97535 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Friday 11am-4pm, Saturday-Sunday noon-4pm Email (541) 774-6654 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE COUNTY ANIMAL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects individuals and animals, works to improve rabies vaccination rates, licenses dogs, enforces laws, and responds to humane complaints. Also operates a shelter that takes in and cares for cats and dogs and adopts out dogs, cats, puppies, and kittens. 1420 Brookside Boulevard Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 10am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 474-5458 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ANIMAL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Houses stray, abandoned, and abused animals. Offers SNIP (Spay Neuter Incentive Program) for cats and SNAP (Spay Neuter Assistance Program) for dogs. Provides pet adoption services, including dogs, cats, and large animals. 4240 Washburn Way Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday noon-4pm (541) 884-7387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINCOLN COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE LINCOLN COUNTY ANIMAL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter for lost, homeless, abused, and neglected animals. Offers pets for adoption. 510 NE Harney Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 265-0720 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE LINN COUNTY SHERIFF’S OFFICE ANIMAL CONTROL ANIMAL CONTROL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a platform to place complaints about animals and reports about lost dogs. Also offers information about adoptable dogs. 3008 SW Ferry Street Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm, closed major holidays (541) 967-3925 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY DOG SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following services: -Enforces dog licensing and control ordinances -Promotes the humane treatment of dogs -Provides shelter and care for stray dogs until they are reunited with their families or adopted -Dog adoption and fostering 3550 Aumsville Highway SE Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Dog Services: Monday-Friday noon-6pm, Saturday 10am-4pm Enforcement and Complaints: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Adoption: Tuesday/Thursday/Friday noon-6pm, Saturday 11am-3pm Email (503) 588-5233 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Picks up injured or aggressive animals and loose livestock, issues dog and cat licenses, investigates animal cruelty, and enforces animal control laws. Accepts stray animals from Multnomah County as space allows, and takes a limited number of owner-surrendered pets. Adoption services include vaccinations, exams, licenses, and spaying/neutering. Also removes dead stray animals and wildlife from public property. Volunteer opportunities are available, and donations are welcome. 1700 W Historic Columbia River Highway Troutdale, OR 97060 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Shelter: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 11am-5:30pm, Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4:30pm Customer Service phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 11am-5:30pm, Wednesday 1pm-4:30pm, Saturday/Sunday 9am-4:30pm Animal-related emergency calls: 7 days per week 8am-5pm. Dial 911 for after-hours animal emergencies. Email (503) 988-7387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is no-kill shelter. There may be a wait time for surrendered/found animals if the shelter is at capacity. The society also has a food-assistance application that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 2840 Rhododendron Drive Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 10am-4pm Email (541) 997-4277 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY PORTLAND CAMPUS ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoption, shelter services, and animal surrender services. 1067 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 972111411 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: 7 days per week 7am-7pm Shelter hours and donations: 7 days per week 10am-7pm Pet meet-and-greets: 7 days per week 10am-6:30pm Email (503) 285-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY SALEM CAMPUS ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoption, shelter services, and animal surrender services. 4246 Turner Road SE Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: 7 days per week 7am-7pm Shelter hours and donations: 7 days per week 10am-7pm Pet meet-and-greets: 7 days per week 10am-6:30pm Email (503) 285-7722 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PAWS ANIMAL SHELTER ANIMAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a no-kill animal shelter specializing in care for orphaned, homeless, and newborn animals. Animals are screened for diseases on intake. Offers adoption and foster opportunities. The shelter has limited capacity and services may not always be available. In these instances, shelter staff may still be able to provide information or assist in directing to other available services in the community. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 650-0855 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PIONEER HUMANE SOCIETY PENDLETON ANIMAL WELFARE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter with opportunities to adopt dogs and cats, all of which have been spayed or neutered. Also provides an animal shelter, and a low-cost spay and neutering clinic. This is a no-kill shelter. 517 SE 3rd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Adoption and Shelter: Unrestricted Neuter/Spay Services: Low-income households Hours: Tuesday-Saturday noon-4pm Email (541) 276-0181 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PIXIE PROJECT ADOPTION AND RESCUE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides adoption services for people looking for cats or dogs, focusing on finding life-long matches. Works with county shelters and rescues to offer support when they become overcrowded. Animals are examined by a veterinarian and thoroughly screened before entering the adoption center. All animals are spayed or neutered, vaccinated, and microchipped. Accepts owner-surrendered cats as time and circumstances allow. Also displays "courtesy posts" of animals needing to be rehomed on website. 510 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Call or visit website for more information Hours: Tuesday-Friday 11am-6pm, Saturday 11am-4pm by appointment Email (503) 542-3432 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill organization. Also offers a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. PO Box 938 Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 460-6420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers adoption of dogs and cats that the humane society takes in, cares for, and houses at the no-kill shelter. All animals that are adopted out have been spayed and neutered. 429 NW Scenic Drive Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday noon-4pm Email (541) 479-5154 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. The society also has a food bank that provides pet food for low-income pet owners. 32220 Old Highway 34 Tangent, OR 973899770 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Animal Intake: by Appointment Only 7 days per week noon-5pm Pet food bank 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 9am-11am Email (541) 928-2789 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an animal shelter that cares for homeless, abandoned, or neglected animals. Offers a pet food pantry when supplies are available and accepts pet food donations. The shelter maintains lost-and-found pet reports for Douglas County and ensures that all adoptable cats and dogs are spayed or neutered and microchipped before adoption. Pets are accepted for surrender only in emergencies and as space allows, and low-cost spay and neuter surgeries are offered on a limited basis. 450 Old Del Rio Road Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday-Saturday noon-7pm Email (541) 672-3907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SECOND CHANCE COMPANIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in finding owners for pets who are currently or about to become homeless, such as if an individual is entering an extended care facility that does not allow animals. Maintains a list of adoptable pets and provides referrals to low-cost spay/neuter clinics. Volunteer opportunities are available. PO Box 2343 Battle Ground, WA 98604 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (360) 687-4569 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL RESCUE AND ADOPTION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers adoption of animals that the Humane Society receives, cares for, and houses at the no-kill shelter. All adopted animals have been spayed and neutered. A foster to adopt program is available for animals that are too young to be spay/neutered. Takes in surrendered animals and animals from high-population shelters. 828 Railroad Street Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 12:30pm-4:30pm Email (541) 412-0325 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an opportunity to adopt animals, all of which have been spayed and neutered. This is a no-kill shelter. 2910 Table Rock Road Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week noon-5pm; except major holidays Email (541) 779-3215 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY RESOURCE CENTER PET ADOPTION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help connecting people who need to relinquish a dog with people who want to adopt. Does not operate a shelter but works to rehome dogs. Dogs will be spayed/neutered (if not already) at the agency's expense before going to a new home. PO Box 651 Winchester, OR 97495 Eligibility: Some larger and specific breeds may not be accepted Hours: 24 hours a day / 7 days a week Email (541) 672-5039 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANIMAL AID INC DOMESTIC ANIMAL SERVICES BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER BRIGHTSIDE ANIMAL CENTER CAT ADOPTION TEAM PET ADOPTIONS CLACKAMAS COUNTY DOG SERVICES DOG SERVICES CLATSOP COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ANIMAL CONTROL COLUMBIA GORGE HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION COLUMBIA HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY HOMEWARD BOUND PETS HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES HOOD RIVER COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE SHERIFF'S OFFICE HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY OF CENTRAL OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY OF EASTERN OREGON PET RESCUE ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES JACKSON COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES JACKSON COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES JOSEPHINE COUNTY ANIMAL SHELTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY ANIMAL SHELTER KLAMATH ANIMAL SHELTER KLAMATH ANIMAL SHELTER LINCOLN COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE LINCOLN COUNTY ANIMAL SHELTER LINN COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ANIMAL CONTROL MARION COUNTY DOG SERVICES MARION COUNTY DOG SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY ANIMAL SERVICES OREGON COAST HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL SHELTER AND ADOPTION SERVICES PIONEER HUMANE SOCIETY PENDLETON ANIMAL WELFARE SHELTER PIXIE PROJECT ADOPTION AND RESCUE ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH ROCKN EZ RESCUE RANCH ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION SAFEHAVEN HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SOUTH COAST HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL RESCUE AND ADOPTION CENTER SOUTHERN OREGON HUMANE SOCIETY ANIMAL ADOPTION UMPQUA VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET ADOPTION ASSISTANCE
